title: ‘The Odyssey’ Proves It: Audiences Want Movies Made the Hard Way
excerpt: This year's breakouts like 'Project Hail Mary' and 'Obsession' were made by filmmakers who sweated. While CG-stuffed franchise disappointments such as 'Supergirl,' 'Moana,' and 'The Mandalorian and Grogu' looked lazy.
content_html:
Audiences in 2026 are sending a clear message to filmmakers: We want movies to look real — and for you to sweat while making them.

The most exciting box office hits so far this year have been projects that emphasized analog filmmaking and had directors going the extra mile to create original cinematic worlds that felt grounded and tactile. 

Meanwhile, several disappointing franchise films — projects with the wind at their backs thanks to built-in audiences — were criticized for looking overtly artificial.

The Odyssey ($264 million and counting), Project Hail Mary ($683 million), Backrooms ($384 million) and Obsession ($442 million) not only heavily relied on practical effects but communicated their old-school moviemaking efforts in their marketing. 

Project Hail Mary directors Phil Lord and Christopher Miller went viral for touting their lack of green screens and use of puppetry (there was also plenty of CG, to be sure). Christopher Nolan and his Odyssey cast have spoken extensively about their herculean efforts to film at tough-to-reach real-world locations, and the director refused to use CG set extensions (any other filmmaker would have been hugely tempted to use CG to make Ithaca look more populated and sprawling, for example).

Even with a modest $10 million budget, the Backrooms team built extensive physical sets and used practical effects whenever possible. And when Curry Barker made Obsession for just $750,000, he had to figure out practical ways of pulling off scares by necessity, if not also by preference.

Nolan has said, “I definitely hit the limits of my own stamina and everybody’s stamina. I mean, it’s The Odyssey, of course it should be difficult. We’re not doing the job right making a film of The Odyssey if it doesn’t seem difficult.” 

But perhaps Nolan was, for once, thinking too narrowly. The real lesson here might be: All filmmaking should be difficult if you want to persuade audiences to leave their homes and trek to a theater. Particularly in the wake of Disney+’s Star Wars and Marvel TV shows, making yet another franchise movie shot on a volume stage feels more like a streaming-channel level of effort than “buy an Imax ticket” effort.

It’s probably not a coincidence that audiences are responding to practicality the same year AI slop has become so widely derided. The trailers for box office disappointments Masters of the Universe, Supergirl, Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu and Moana all notably looked, well, a bit fakey.

Supergirl was derided for its effects and action slop. Moana was supposed to be a live-action remake, but could you even tell? In the wake of Project Hail Mary’s practical effects headlines, the team behind The Mandalorian and Grogu put out a “Hey, we did that, too!” video touting the Star Wars film’s practical puppet work, but the movie’s trailers and clips looked slathered in CG glaze.

Moviegoers will break box office records to see Avengers: Doomsday, yet even that highly anticipated film’s trailer released July 20 is being criticized as looking like the actors were trapped on a soundstage. (One viral Reddit comment asked: “Is anyone else concerned how ‘filmed in a cupboard’ this looks?” while an influencer opined, “[The superhero genre] is supposed to be all about spectacle, and this no longer counts as spectacle”)

It used to be that only cinephiles cared how a movie was made. But in our authenticity era — when anybody can create a short AI film by typing prompts — the story behind the film’s creation is gaining value. This is especially true when that story conveys some real sweat equity (such as A-list actors hiking 45 minutes up a hill every day to reach an Italian fortress to shoot The Odyssey).

To some extent, audiences have always loved tales of Hollywood creatives suffering for their art. David Lean shooting Lawrence of Arabia almost entirely on location in the unforgiving deserts of Jordan, Morocco and Spain. Steven Spielberg nearly killing himself — and nearly getting fired — to make Jaws. James Cameron risking ruin to go big with Titanic. But those production tales were appreciated after those movies were a success. Now you have to tell a compelling story — and tell a compelling story about telling your story. 

The other side of this coin is the refreshing surge in popularity of nonfranchise movies. 

Last year, 12 of the top 13 movies at the domestic box office were titles based on existing IP — franchise movies, sequels, video game adaptations and the like (Sinners was the only exception).

What an incredible turn of events this year. So far, five of the top 10 movies at the domestic box office are nonfranchise titles — and The Odyssey will quickly join those ranks.

Declaring Hollywood trends is a perilous business. Every movie’s success is a combination of factors, and just when you’re certain that there’s been real change, things tend to revert to the norm. Is the superhero fatigue virus really mutating into franchise fatigue? Too soon to say.

But the tea leaves, as best they can be read, seem pretty clear: Moviegoers will show up in droves for compelling nonfranchise stories from passionate filmmakers who can give audiences a feeling of handcrafted reality. 

This story appeared in the July 22 issue of The Hollywood Reporter magazine. Click here to subscribe.

blocks.html_en: Audiences in 2026 are sending a clear message to filmmakers: We want movies to look real — and for you to sweat while making them.

sentences.en: Audiences in 2026 are sending a clear message to filmmakers: We want movies to look real — and for you to sweat while making them.
sentences.zh: 2026 年的观众正在向电影制作人传达一个明确的信息：我们希望电影看起来真实，并且让您在制作过程中流汗。

blocks.html_en: The most exciting box office hits so far this year have been projects that emphasized analog filmmaking and had directors going the extra mile to create original cinematic worlds that felt grounded and tactile.

sentences.en: The most exciting box office hits so far this year have been projects that emphasized analog filmmaking and had directors going the extra mile to create original cinematic worlds that felt grounded and tactile.
sentences.zh: 今年迄今为止最令人兴奋的票房大片都是强调模拟电影制作的项目，并且导演们不遗余力地创造出脚踏实地、有触觉的原创电影世界。

blocks.html_en: Meanwhile, several disappointing franchise films — projects with the wind at their backs thanks to built-in audiences — were criticized for looking overtly artificial.

sentences.en: Meanwhile, several disappointing franchise films — projects with the wind at their backs thanks to built-in audiences — were criticized for looking overtly artificial.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，几部令人失望的系列电影——由于内置观众而在背后支持的项目——因看起来过于做作而受到批评。

blocks.html_en: The Odyssey ($264 million and counting), Project Hail Mary ($683 million), Backrooms ($384 million) and Obsession ($442 million) not only heavily relied on practical effects but communicated their old-school moviemaking efforts in their marketing.

sentences.en: The Odyssey ($264 million and counting), Project Hail Mary ($683 million), Backrooms ($384 million) and Obsession ($442 million) not only heavily relied on practical effects but communicated their old-school moviemaking efforts in their marketing.
sentences.zh: 《奥德赛》（2.64 亿美元，还在增加）、《万福玛丽计划》（6.83 亿美元）、《密室》（3.84 亿美元）和《痴迷》（4.42 亿美元）不仅严重依赖实际效果，而且在营销中传达了他们老派电影制作的努力。

blocks.html_en: Project Hail Mary directors Phil Lord and Christopher Miller went viral for touting their lack of green screens and use of puppetry (there was also plenty of CG, to be sure). Christopher Nolan and his Odyssey cast have spoken extensively about their herculean efforts to film at tough-to-reach real-world locations, and the director refused to use CG set extensions (any other filmmaker would have been hugely tempted to use CG to make Ithaca look more populated and sprawling, for example).

sentences.en: Project Hail Mary directors Phil Lord and Christopher Miller went viral for touting their lack of green screens and use of puppetry (there was also plenty of CG, to be sure).
sentences.zh: 《万福玛丽》的导演菲尔·洛德和克里斯托弗·米勒因宣传他们没有绿幕和使用木偶（当然也有大量的 CG）而走红。

sentences.en: Christopher Nolan and his Odyssey cast have spoken extensively about their herculean efforts to film at tough-to-reach real-world locations, and the director refused to use CG set extensions (any other filmmaker would have been hugely tempted to use CG to make Ithaca look more populated and sprawling, for example).
sentences.zh: 克里斯托弗·诺兰和他的《奥德赛》演员们广泛谈论了他们在难以到达的现实世界地点进行拍摄的巨大努力，而导演拒绝使用 CG 布景扩展（例如，任何其他电影制作人都会非常想使用 CG 来让伊萨卡岛看起来更拥挤、更广阔）。

blocks.html_en: Even with a modest $10 million budget, the Backrooms team built extensive physical sets and used practical effects whenever possible. And when Curry Barker made Obsession for just $750,000, he had to figure out practical ways of pulling off scares by necessity, if not also by preference.

sentences.en: Even with a modest $10 million budget, the Backrooms team built extensive physical sets and used practical effects whenever possible.
sentences.zh: 尽管预算只有 1000 万美元，但 Backrooms 团队仍然构建了大量的物理场景，并尽可能使用实际效果。

sentences.en: And when Curry Barker made Obsession for just $750,000, he had to figure out practical ways of pulling off scares by necessity, if not also by preference.
sentences.zh: 当库里·巴克 (Curry Barker) 以仅 75 万美元的价格制作《痴迷》时，他必须找出切实可行的方法来消除恐惧，即使不是出于偏好，也是必要的。

blocks.html_en: Nolan has said, “I definitely hit the limits of my own stamina and everybody’s stamina. I mean, it’s The Odyssey, of course it should be difficult. We’re not doing the job right making a film of The Odyssey if it doesn’t seem difficult.”

sentences.en: Nolan has said, “I definitely hit the limits of my own stamina and everybody’s stamina.
sentences.zh: 诺兰曾表示：“我确实达到了自己和每个人的耐力极限。

sentences.en: I mean, it’s The Odyssey, of course it should be difficult.
sentences.zh: 我的意思是，这是《奥德赛》，当然应该很困难。

sentences.en: We’re not doing the job right making a film of The Odyssey if it doesn’t seem difficult.”
sentences.zh: 如果《奥德赛》的电影看起来不困难的话，我们就没有做好这项工作。”

blocks.html_en: But perhaps Nolan was, for once, thinking too narrowly. The real lesson here might be: All filmmaking should be difficult if you want to persuade audiences to leave their homes and trek to a theater. Particularly in the wake of Disney+’s Star Wars and Marvel TV shows, making yet another franchise movie shot on a volume stage feels more like a streaming-channel level of effort than “buy an Imax ticket” effort.

sentences.en: But perhaps Nolan was, for once, thinking too narrowly.
sentences.zh: 但也许诺兰这一次想得太狭隘了。

sentences.en: The real lesson here might be: All filmmaking should be difficult if you want to persuade audiences to leave their homes and trek to a theater.
sentences.zh: 这里真正的教训可能是：如果你想说服观众离开家长途跋涉去剧院，所有电影制作都应该很困难。

sentences.en: Particularly in the wake of Disney+’s Star Wars and Marvel TV shows, making yet another franchise movie shot on a volume stage feels more like a streaming-channel level of effort than “buy an Imax ticket” effort.
sentences.zh: 特别是在 Disney+ 的《星球大战》和漫威电视节目推出之后，制作另一部在批量舞台上拍摄的系列电影感觉更像是流媒体频道层面的努力，而不是“购买 Imax 门票”的努力。

blocks.html_en: It’s probably not a coincidence that audiences are responding to practicality the same year AI slop has become so widely derided. The trailers for box office disappointments Masters of the Universe, Supergirl, Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu and Moana all notably looked, well, a bit fakey.

sentences.en: It’s probably not a coincidence that audiences are responding to practicality the same year AI slop has become so widely derided.
sentences.zh: 在人工智能的废话受到广泛嘲笑的同一年，观众对实用性做出了反应，这可能并非巧合。

sentences.en: The trailers for box office disappointments Masters of the Universe, Supergirl, Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu and Moana all notably looked, well, a bit fakey.
sentences.zh: 票房令人失望的《宇宙大师》、《女超人》、《星球大战：曼达洛人》和《格罗古与莫阿娜》的预告片看起来都显得有点假。

blocks.html_en: Supergirl was derided for its effects and action slop. Moana was supposed to be a live-action remake, but could you even tell? In the wake of Project Hail Mary’s practical effects headlines, the team behind The Mandalorian and Grogu put out a “Hey, we did that, too!” video touting the Star Wars film’s practical puppet work, but the movie’s trailers and clips looked slathered in CG glaze.

sentences.en: Supergirl was derided for its effects and action slop.
sentences.zh: 《女超人》因其特效和动作拖沓而受到嘲笑。

sentences.en: Moana was supposed to be a live-action remake, but could you even tell?
sentences.zh: 《莫阿娜》本来应该是真人翻拍版，但你能看出来吗？

sentences.en: In the wake of Project Hail Mary’s practical effects headlines, the team behind The Mandalorian and Grogu put out a “Hey, we did that, too!”
sentences.zh: 在《万福玛丽计划》的实际效果成为头条新闻后，《曼达洛人》和《格罗古》背后的团队发布了一句“嘿，我们也这么做了！”

sentences.en: video touting the Star Wars film’s practical puppet work, but the movie’s trailers and clips looked slathered in CG glaze.
sentences.zh: 宣传《星球大战》电影实用木偶作品的视频，但电影的预告片和剪辑看起来充满了 CG 效果。

blocks.html_en: Moviegoers will break box office records to see Avengers: Doomsday, yet even that highly anticipated film’s trailer released July 20 is being criticized as looking like the actors were trapped on a soundstage. (One viral Reddit comment asked: “Is anyone else concerned how ‘filmed in a cupboard’ this looks?” while an influencer opined, “[The superhero genre] is supposed to be all about spectacle, and this no longer counts as spectacle”)

sentences.en: Moviegoers will break box office records to see Avengers: Doomsday, yet even that highly anticipated film’s trailer released July 20 is being criticized as looking like the actors were trapped on a soundstage.
sentences.zh: 电影观众观看《复仇者联盟：世界末日》将会打破票房纪录，但即使是这部备受期待的电影 7 月 20 日发布的预告片也因演员们被困在摄影棚中而受到批评。

sentences.en: (One viral Reddit comment asked: “Is anyone else concerned how ‘filmed in a cupboard’ this looks?”
sentences.zh: （Reddit 上的一条评论问道：“还有人关心这个‘在橱柜里拍摄’的样子吗？”

sentences.en: while an influencer opined, “[The superhero genre] is supposed to be all about spectacle, and this no longer counts as spectacle”)
sentences.zh: 而一位有影响力的人认为，“[超级英雄类型]应该是奇观，而这不再算作奇观”）

blocks.html_en: It used to be that only cinephiles cared how a movie was made. But in our authenticity era — when anybody can create a short AI film by typing prompts — the story behind the film’s creation is gaining value. This is especially true when that story conveys some real sweat equity (such as A-list actors hiking 45 minutes up a hill every day to reach an Italian fortress to shoot The Odyssey).

sentences.en: It used to be that only cinephiles cared how a movie was made.
sentences.zh: 过去，只有影迷才关心电影是如何制作的。

sentences.en: But in our authenticity era — when anybody can create a short AI film by typing prompts — the story behind the film’s creation is gaining value.
sentences.zh: 但在我们的真实时代——任何人都可以通过输入提示来创作一部人工智能短片——电影创作背后的故事正在获得价值。

sentences.en: This is especially true when that story conveys some real sweat equity (such as A-list actors hiking 45 minutes up a hill every day to reach an Italian fortress to shoot The Odyssey).
sentences.zh: 当这个故事传达了一些真正的汗水资产时尤其如此（例如一线演员每天徒步 45 分钟上山到达意大利堡垒拍摄《奥德赛》）。

blocks.html_en: To some extent, audiences have always loved tales of Hollywood creatives suffering for their art. David Lean shooting Lawrence of Arabia almost entirely on location in the unforgiving deserts of Jordan, Morocco and Spain. But those production tales were appreciated after those movies were a success. Now you have to tell a compelling story — and tell a compelling story about telling your story.

sentences.en: To some extent, audiences have always loved tales of Hollywood creatives suffering for their art.
sentences.zh: 在某种程度上，观众一直喜欢好莱坞创意人士因艺术而受苦的故事。

sentences.en: David Lean shooting Lawrence of Arabia almost entirely on location in the unforgiving deserts of Jordan, Morocco and Spain.
sentences.zh: 大卫·里恩的《阿拉伯的劳伦斯》几乎全部在约旦、摩洛哥和西班牙的无情沙漠中拍摄。

sentences.en: Steven Spielberg nearly killing himself — and nearly getting fired — to make Jaws.
sentences.zh: 史蒂文·斯皮尔伯格为了拍《大白鲨》差点自杀，还差点被解雇。

sentences.en: James Cameron risking ruin to go big with Titanic.
sentences.zh: 詹姆斯·卡梅隆冒着破产的风险去拍摄《泰坦尼克号》。

sentences.en: But those production tales were appreciated after those movies were a success.
sentences.zh: 但在这些电影取得成功后，这些制作故事受到了赞赏。

sentences.en: Now you have to tell a compelling story — and tell a compelling story about telling your story.
sentences.zh: 现在你必须讲述一个引人入胜的故事——并讲述一个关于讲述你的故事的引人入胜的故事。

blocks.html_en: The other side of this coin is the refreshing surge in popularity of nonfranchise movies.

sentences.en: The other side of this coin is the refreshing surge in popularity of nonfranchise movies.
sentences.zh: 这枚硬币的另一面是非特许经营电影的受欢迎程度令人耳目一新的激增。

blocks.html_en: Last year, 12 of the top 13 movies at the domestic box office were titles based on existing IP — franchise movies, sequels, video game adaptations and the like (Sinners was the only exception).

sentences.en: Last year, 12 of the top 13 movies at the domestic box office were titles based on existing IP — franchise movies, sequels, video game adaptations and the like (Sinners was the only exception).
sentences.zh: 去年，国内票房前 13 名的电影中有 12 部是基于现有 IP 的电影——特许经营电影、续集、视频游戏改编电影等（《罪人》是唯一的例外）。

blocks.html_en: What an incredible turn of events this year. So far, five of the top 10 movies at the domestic box office are nonfranchise titles — and The Odyssey will quickly join those ranks.

sentences.en: What an incredible turn of events this year.
sentences.zh: 今年发生的事情真是令人难以置信。

sentences.en: So far, five of the top 10 movies at the domestic box office are nonfranchise titles — and The Odyssey will quickly join those ranks.

sentences.en: Declaring Hollywood trends is a perilous business.
sentences.zh: 宣扬好莱坞潮流是一件危险的事情。

sentences.en: Every movie’s success is a combination of factors, and just when you’re certain that there’s been real change, things tend to revert to the norm.
sentences.zh: 每部电影的成功都是多种因素的综合作用，当你确定发生了真正的变化时，事情往往会回归常态。

sentences.en: Is the superhero fatigue virus really mutating into franchise fatigue?
sentences.zh: 超级英雄疲劳病毒真的会变异为特许经营疲劳吗？

sentences.en: Too soon to say.
sentences.zh: 现在说还为时过早。

blocks.html_en: But the tea leaves, as best they can be read, seem pretty clear: Moviegoers will show up in droves for compelling nonfranchise stories from passionate filmmakers who can give audiences a feeling of handcrafted reality.

sentences.en: But the tea leaves, as best they can be read, seem pretty clear: Moviegoers will show up in droves for compelling nonfranchise stories from passionate filmmakers who can give audiences a feeling of handcrafted reality.
sentences.zh: 但从最好的角度来看，事情似乎很清楚：电影观众会成群结队地观看由充满激情的电影制作人创作的引人入胜的非特许经营故事，这些故事可以给观众一种手工制作的现实感。

content_html_zh:
2026 年的观众正在向电影制作人传达一个明确的信息：我们希望电影看起来真实，并且让您在制作过程中流汗。

今年迄今为止最令人兴奋的票房大片都是强调模拟电影制作的项目，并且导演们不遗余力地创造出脚踏实地、有触觉的原创电影世界。

与此同时，几部令人失望的系列电影——由于内置观众而在背后支持的项目——因看起来过于做作而受到批评。

《奥德赛》（2.64 亿美元，还在增加）、《万福玛丽计划》（6.83 亿美元）、《密室》（3.84 亿美元）和《痴迷》（4.42 亿美元）不仅严重依赖实际效果，而且在营销中传达了他们老派电影制作的努力。

《万福玛丽》的导演菲尔·洛德和克里斯托弗·米勒因宣传他们没有绿幕和使用木偶（当然也有大量的 CG）而走红。 克里斯托弗·诺兰和他的《奥德赛》演员们广泛谈论了他们在难以到达的现实世界地点进行拍摄的巨大努力，而导演拒绝使用 CG 布景扩展（例如，任何其他电影制作人都会非常想使用 CG 来让伊萨卡岛看起来更拥挤、更广阔）。

尽管预算只有 1000 万美元，但 Backrooms 团队仍然构建了大量的物理场景，并尽可能使用实际效果。 当库里·巴克 (Curry Barker) 以仅 75 万美元的价格制作《痴迷》时，他必须找出切实可行的方法来消除恐惧，即使不是出于偏好，也是必要的。

诺兰曾表示：“我确实达到了自己和每个人的耐力极限。 我的意思是，这是《奥德赛》，当然应该很困难。 如果《奥德赛》的电影看起来不困难的话，我们就没有做好这项工作。”

但也许诺兰这一次想得太狭隘了。 这里真正的教训可能是：如果你想说服观众离开家长途跋涉去剧院，所有电影制作都应该很困难。 特别是在 Disney+ 的《星球大战》和漫威电视节目推出之后，制作另一部在批量舞台上拍摄的系列电影感觉更像是流媒体频道层面的努力，而不是“购买 Imax 门票”的努力。

在人工智能的废话受到广泛嘲笑的同一年，观众对实用性做出了反应，这可能并非巧合。 票房令人失望的《宇宙大师》、《女超人》、《星球大战：曼达洛人》和《格罗古与莫阿娜》的预告片看起来都显得有点假。

《女超人》因其特效和动作拖沓而受到嘲笑。 《莫阿娜》本来应该是真人翻拍版，但你能看出来吗？ 在《万福玛丽计划》的实际效果成为头条新闻后，《曼达洛人》和《格罗古》背后的团队发布了一句“嘿，我们也这么做了！” 宣传《星球大战》电影实用木偶作品的视频，但电影的预告片和剪辑看起来充满了 CG 效果。

电影观众观看《复仇者联盟：世界末日》将会打破票房纪录，但即使是这部备受期待的电影 7 月 20 日发布的预告片也因演员们被困在摄影棚中而受到批评。 （Reddit 上的一条评论问道：“还有人关心这个‘在橱柜里拍摄’的样子吗？” 而一位有影响力的人认为，“[超级英雄类型]应该是奇观，而这不再算作奇观”）

过去，只有影迷才关心电影是如何制作的。 但在我们的真实时代——任何人都可以通过输入提示来创作一部人工智能短片——电影创作背后的故事正在获得价值。 当这个故事传达了一些真正的汗水资产时尤其如此（例如一线演员每天徒步 45 分钟上山到达意大利堡垒拍摄《奥德赛》）。

在某种程度上，观众一直喜欢好莱坞创意人士因艺术而受苦的故事。 大卫·里恩的《阿拉伯的劳伦斯》几乎全部在约旦、摩洛哥和西班牙的无情沙漠中拍摄。 史蒂文·斯皮尔伯格为了拍《大白鲨》差点自杀，还差点被解雇。 詹姆斯·卡梅隆冒着破产的风险去拍摄《泰坦尼克号》。 但在这些电影取得成功后，这些制作故事受到了赞赏。 现在你必须讲述一个引人入胜的故事——并讲述一个关于讲述你的故事的引人入胜的故事。

这枚硬币的另一面是非特许经营电影的受欢迎程度令人耳目一新的激增。

去年，国内票房前 13 名的电影中有 12 部是基于现有 IP 的电影——特许经营电影、续集、视频游戏改编电影等（《罪人》是唯一的例外）。

今年发生的事情真是令人难以置信。 到目前为止，国内票房前十的电影中有五部是非特许经营电影——《奥德赛》将很快加入这一行列。

宣扬好莱坞潮流是一件危险的事情。 每部电影的成功都是多种因素的综合作用，当你确定发生了真正的变化时，事情往往会回归常态。 超级英雄疲劳病毒真的会变异为特许经营疲劳吗？ 现在说还为时过早。

但从最好的角度来看，事情似乎很清楚：电影观众会成群结队地观看由充满激情的电影制作人创作的引人入胜的非特许经营故事，这些故事可以给观众一种手工制作的现实感。

这个故事出现在 7 月 22 日的《好莱坞报道》杂志上。 单击此处订阅。
